title: Badge
compact: How to test a badge
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the control with a badge (like normal)
- Space or enter: activates any controls (like normal)
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the control with a badge (like normal)
- Doubletap: activates any controls (like normal)
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the name of the control, then badge content (Ex: Home, 1 new notification)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/badge/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a badge
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control with a badge I SEE focus is visually indicated on the entire control NOT the badge
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control with a badge
- I HEAR the name of the control, then badge content (Ex: Home, 1 new notification)
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/badge/

title: Bottom app bar
compact: How to test a bottom app bar
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: Activates the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the button, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear and matches any visible label
- Role: It identifies itself as a button
- Group: Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/bottom-app-bar/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a bottom app bar
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the action occurs
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button
- I HEAR Its purpose is clear and matches any visible label
- I HEAR It identifies itself as a button
- I HEAR Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- I HEAR It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I HEAR the action occurs
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/bottom-app-bar/

title: Floating action button
compact: How to test a floating action button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: Activates the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear
- Role: It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
- Group: Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-floating/

title: Icon button
compact: How to test an icon button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: Activates the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear
- Role: It identifies itself as a button or "double tap to activate"
- Group: When applicable, a tooltip is available to help describe the icon button's purpose
- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/button-icon/

title: Card
compact: How to test a card
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to any control inside the card box
- Spacebar or enter: the control is selected
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to any element inside the cardbox
- Doubletap: intended actions occur
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role actionable: if non-actionable, any nested control expresses its role
- Role non-actionable: if directly actionable, it identifies as button or link
- State: any nested control expresses its state
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/card/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a card
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to any nested control I SEE focus is strongly visually indicated
- THEN when I use the spacebar or enter to activate the control I SEE the action occurs
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to any nested control
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- I HEAR if non-actionable, any nested control expresses its role
- I HEAR if directly actionable, it identifies as button or link
- I HEAR any nested control expresses its state
- THEN when I use the spacebar or enter to activate the control I HEAR the action occurs
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/card/

title: Carousel
compact: How to test a carousel
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: moves to the previous or next carousel item
- Spacebar or enter: activates the focused carousel item
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the next slide or controls
- Single point control: focus moves to the next slide (ex: Next button)
- Doubletap: activates the slides or controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Carousel name: the purpose of the carousel is clear
- Carousel role: it identifies as container
- Carousel slide: the purpose of the slide is clear
- Slide role: it identifies as list item, index (ex: 3 of 5)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/carousel/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a carousel

- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the slide I SEE the action occurs
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a slide
- I HEAR the purpose of the carousel is clear
- I HEAR it identifies as container
- I HEAR the purpose of the slide is clear
- I HEAR it identifies as list item, index (ex: 3 of 5)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the slide I HEAR the action occurs
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/carousel/

- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to the checkbox

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: element toggles between states
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a checkbox
- Group: any visible label is read with the checkbox in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected, unselected, disabled)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information

title: Chips
compact: How to test a chips
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to the chip
- Spacebar or enter: activates the chip
- Backspace or delete: removes current focused input chip
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the chip, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: activates the chip
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button, radio button or checkbox
- Group: any visible label is read with the chip in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (checked, unchecked, disabled, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/chips/

title: Date picker
compact: How to test a date picker
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to a date
- Spacebar or enter: activates the control
- Page up/down: move to the same date on next/previous month
- Home/end: move to the first day of the
- Shift + page up/down: moves to the same date in the next/previous year
- Shift + m: moves to the month list dropdown
- Shift + y: moves to the year list dropdown
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the control
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches or extends visible label
- Role: each item identifies its role (combo box for text input, button, full date for selection ex: "17" = "Monday, August 17")
- Group: each component is a separately focusable item
- State: each item can expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/date-picker/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a date picker
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab key to move focus to an individual control (text input, button, date) I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I SEE the selection is made
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key to move focus to an individual control (text input, button, date)
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches or extends visible label
- I HEAR each item identifies its role (combo box for text input, button, full date for selection ex: "17" = "Monday, August 17")
- I HEAR each component is a separately focusable item
- I HEAR each item can expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter to activate the button I HEAR the selection is made
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/date-picker/

title: Full screen dialog
compact: How to test a full screen dialog
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Launch button: focus visibly moves to the open dialog itself
- Arrow keys: content within the dialog is browsed in meaningful order
- Tab: focus visibly moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- Escape: if dismissible, the dialog closes and returns focus to the button that launched it
- Space or enter: any buttons are activated
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within the dialog and doesn't enter the rest of the page
- Doubletap: activates controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- Role: it identifies itself as a dialog
- Group: when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- State: when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/dialog-full-screen/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a full screen dialog
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to the launch button AND THEN use spacebar to activate the button I SEE the dialog opens
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I SEE content in the dialog is browsed in meaningful order and does not leave the dialog
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE focus moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- THEN when I use  I SEE focus returns to the launch button
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to the launch button AND THEN use spacebar to activate the button
- I HEAR the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a dialog
- I HEAR when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- I HEAR when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I HEAR content in the dialog is browsed in meaningful order and does not leave the dialog
- THEN when I use the tab key I HEAR focus moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- THEN when I use  I HEAR focus returns to the launch button
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/dialog-full-screen/

- Doubletap: activates controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices

- Group: when closed, focus returns to launch button or meaningful next step
- State: when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
4. Device OS settings

title: Heading
compact: How to test a heading
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: browses to headings
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: the screenreader reads the heading (and if used, its level)
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it identifies itself as a heading
- Group: (if heading levels are used) it is logically structured
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/heading/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a heading
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the arrow key to browse to a heading I SEE the heading comes into view
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE nothing happens because headings must NOT be focusable
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the arrow key to browse to a heading
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a heading
- I HEAR (if heading levels are used) it is logically structured
- THEN when I use the tab key I HEAR nothing happens because headings must NOT be focusable
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/heading/

title: Decorative image
compact: How to test a decorative image
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: ignores/skips the image completely
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: ignores/skips the image completely
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: it ignores/skips the image completely
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/image-decorative/

- WHEN I use a screenreader AND

title: Number input field
compact: How to test a number input field
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab, arrow keys or ctrl-tab: focus visibly moves to the input
- Spacebar: input can be edited
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the input, expresses its name, role, state then any instructions
- Doubletap: numeric keyboard appears appears to edit
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as text field
- Group: its visible label and instructions are read with the input in focus
- State: if applicable, it expresses its state (required, dimmed, invalid)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: Text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/input-number/

2 - Desktop screenreader

- I use the ctrl + tab or arrow keys to move focus to the input
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as text field
- I HEAR its visible label and instructions are read with the input in focus
- I HEAR if applicable, it expresses its state (required, dimmed, invalid)
3. Device OS settings

Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/input-number/

- I HEAR identifies itself as text field
- I HEAR visible label and instructions are read with the input in focus
- I HEAR if applicable, it expresses its state (required, dimmed, invalid)
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: Text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/input-text/

title: Menu
compact: How to test a menu
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the menu and options
- Up/down arrows: opens closed menus, for open menus moves focus to the next item
- Left/right arrows: opens or closes a submenu
- Letters: focus moves to the next option starting with letter
- Spacebar or enter: opens the menu or selects options
- Escape: closes menu, focus returns to meaningful location
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the menu or options, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: opens the menu or selects options
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe, icons are ignored
- State: it expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/menu/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a menu
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a menu I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I SEE the menu opens
- THEN when I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to an option I SEE focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I SEE the option is selected
- THEN when I use  I SEE the menu closes, focus returns to meaningful location
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a menu
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a button
- I HEAR any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe, icons are ignored
- I HEAR it expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I HEAR the menu opens
- THEN when I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to an option I HEAR focus is visually indicated
- THEN when I use spacebar or enter I HEAR the option is selected
- THEN when I use  I HEAR the menu closes, focus returns to meaningful location
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/menu/

title: Radio button
compact: How to test a radio button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the checked radio button in the group. If a radio button is not checked, focus moves to the first radio button in the group
- Spacebar: if the radio button with focus is not selected, changes the state to selected
- Arrow-keys: moves focus to and selects the previous or next radio button in the group
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: if the radio button with focus is not selected, changes the state to selected. Otherwise, does nothing.
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a radio button
- Group: any visible label is read with the radio button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected, unselected, disabled)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/radio/

- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the radio button I SEE the state is changed
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a radio button
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a radio button
- I HEAR any visible label is read with the radio button in a single swipe
- I HEAR it expresses its state (selected, unselected, disabled)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the radio button I HEAR the state is changed
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/radio/

title: Segmented button
compact: How to test a segmented button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar or enter: selects the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the buttons, expresses its state
- Doubletap: selects the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role single select: if single select it identifies as radio button
- Role multiple select: if multiple select it identifies as checkbox
- Group: its visible label (if any) is grouped or associated with the button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected/disabled)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: this element is exempt from text resizing requirements
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/segmented-button/

2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key to move focus to a segmented button
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR if single select it identifies as radio button
- I HEAR if multiple select it identifies as checkbox

- I HEAR it expresses its state (selected/disabled)

3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: this element is exempt from text resizing requirements
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/segmented-button/

title: Sheet
compact: How to test a sheet
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Launch button: focus visibly moves to the sheet
- Space or enter: sheet toggles between available heights
- Tab: starting with the drag handle, focus visibly moves to interactive controls in the sheet
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within the sheet
- Doubletap: activates most elements
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the sheet describes its purpose or title
- Role: it identifies itself as a bottom sheet or side sheet
- Group: when closed, focus returns to the launch button
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/sheet/

1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use an action that opens a sheet I SEE focus moves to the sheet
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I SEE content in the sheet is browsed in meaningful order starting with the drag handle
- THEN when I use the tab key I SEE focus moves to interactive controls in the sheet in meaningful order starting with the drag handle
- THEN when I use  I SEE the sheet toggles between available heights
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use an action that opens a sheet
- I HEAR the sheet describes its purpose or title
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a bottom sheet or side sheet
- I HEAR when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I HEAR content in the sheet is browsed in meaningful order starting with the drag handle
- THEN when I use the tab key I HEAR focus moves to interactive controls in the sheet in meaningful order starting with the drag handle
- THEN when I use  I HEAR the sheet toggles between available heights
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/sheet/

title: Slider
compact: How to test a slider
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab: focus visibly moves to the slider handle
- Arrow keys: increases and decreases the value by one
- Spacebar + arrow keys: increase and decrease the value by one interval or one stop indicator
- Home/end: set the slider to the first and last values on the slider
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the slider, expresses its name, role, state
- Swipe up/down: increases and decreases the value by one
- Doubletap + hold + drag right or left: increases and decreases the value by one
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a slider
- State: it expresses its value
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/slider/

2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab key or arrow key to move focus to a slider handle
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a slider
- I HEAR it expresses its value
- THEN when I use the arrow keys  I HEAR the value increases or decreases by one
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/slider/

title: Snackbar
compact: How to test a snackbar
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Triggering action: focus does not move to the snackbar
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the snackbar at the end of the screen
- Space or enter: activates any controls in the snackbar
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the snackbar at the end of the screen
- Doubletap: activates any controls in the snackbar
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the snackbar text is announced on appearing
- Group: when dismissed manually, focus returns to meaningful next step
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/snackbar/

title: Time picker
compact: How to test a time picker
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to a time slot

- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: It identifies its role (text input, button, checkbox)

- State: It expresses its state (disabled, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/time-picker/

title: Tooltip (rich)
compact: How to test a tooltip (rich)
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or tab: moves focus to the control, causing the rich tooltip to display
- Tab or arrow keys: moves focus any controls in the rich tooltip
- Space or enter: activates any controls
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to a control causing the rich tooltip to be announced, moves focus through the  tooltip
- Doubletap: activates any controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the content of the rich tooltip is announced after the control
- Role: the control identifies its role (ex: Button)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/tooltip-rich/

- THEN when I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control inside a rich tooltip I SEE focus is visually indicated on the control
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control with a rich tooltip
- I HEAR the content of the rich tooltip is announced after the control
- I HEAR the control identifies its role (ex: Button)
- THEN when I use the tab or arrow keys to move focus to a control inside a rich tooltip I HEAR focus is visually indicated on the control
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/tooltip-rich/

- Space or enter: activates any controls
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the control, causing the plain tooltip to display
- Doubletap: activates any controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the name of the control, matching the plain tooltip (but not both)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-android/tooltip/
